what_it_does: This file is a code-grounded deep read of the Company's local-model layer — fabric, runtime/cognition.py, roles, and the VRAM resource manager — framed through Tim's 'bigger local-model intention' lens. It inventories existing model-binding, embedding, mining, clustering, and resource-governance seams so the merged memory build knows what it can lean on and what must scale.
observations: Embeddings use a dedicated endpoint at localhost:8001 with BAAI/bge-m3, 1024-dim, served by vLLM in pooling mode, separate from chat endpoints.
The embed path is guarded by complete_embeddings in fabric/client.py, which fails loud on transport errors, empty results, count mismatch, and dim mismatch.
One embed plumbing serves both run_role(op='embed') and the batch capture path embed_corpus_to_spaces / vector_index.build_index; single-shot embeds raise, batch embeds degrade-with-warning.
Vector spaces are file-discovered from projections/<id>.py declaring embeds:true, addressed as vec://<item>#space=<id>, and read back by find_relations.
A larger embedder tier, Qwen/Qwen3-Embedding-8B, is already catalogued in ops/model_capabilities.json and services.json as embed-qwen3 (~9 GB).
The resident worker is chat-4b (cyankiwi/Qwen3.5-4B-AWQ-4bit) at :8000, ~7 GB, max_num_seqs 34, with a measured KV profile.
runtime/cognition.py provides a map/reduce engine: run_items fans one role over many units concurrently with per-unit failure isolation; run_reduce supports role/rule/cluster modes.
roles/mine_exchange.py is the transcript-miner map role, producing a schema-enforced MineExchangeOut with a no-fiction floor and a judge_mining re-check.
SlotBudget.from_registry() computes swarm width from live services.json (max_num_seqs and KV pool), bounded by a global VRAM gate.
ops/cli/gpu.py is the single VRAM authority, reading live nvidia-smi, planning eviction, and doing orphan-safe cgroup teardown; capabilities.py ensure_resident provides governed swap approval.
The wake combo (bridge + chat-4b + embed-bge + stt-whisper + tts-kokoro) is cited as ~12 GB co-resident, showing the brain and embedder already run side-by-side.
A constraints table lists the 16 GB ceiling and the non-co-residency of qwen3-8B embedder with the 4B brain.

Six named integration points map merge work onto existing seams: embed at scale, mine every exchange, cluster/consolidate, resource-gate bulk work, capability-query models, and the shared bge-m3 endpoint.
Section 5 expands Tim's intention into continuous re-embedding, always-on mining, scheduled consolidation/dream phases, and on-device cognition over the merged store.

Open questions highlight VRAM as the hard ceiling, three embedder dims needing migration, the local-resident-always swarm policy, unpersisted reduce output, and gpu_util-variant swaps as operator actions.
standouts: The same bge-m3 @ :8001 endpoint is already used by both fabric embeddings and substrate-mcp, making the embedder a pre-shared seam.
run_items and run_reduce already constitute the mining and consolidation substrate the merge wants; the mechanism is built but has only run at sample scale.
The resource manager enforces a governed swap-approval ASK before eviction, so bigger passes can be scheduled without silent displacement.
The 16 GB card is the binding constraint: a fabric-grade qwen3-8B embedder (~9 GB) cannot co-reside with the resident 4B brain (~7 GB), so re-embedding becomes a phased eviction event.
The file's core claim is that the merge needs volume, continuity, and tier — not new model-layer architecture.
conventions: Uses an evidence convention with Observed, Inferred, and Verified labels, and explicitly notes there are no Verified claims because nothing was run live.
Distinguishes what is in the code today from tentative expansions of Tim's intention.
Companion-read structure: this is one of several reads (spine, store, substrate-mcp, conversation-intelligence).
Uses tables to summarize resource constraints and integration points.
File-local and neutral: no ranking of components or declaring winners.
concerns: No claims were verified by live execution; 'verified' assertions are relayed from the code/constitutions, not independently confirmed.
Section 5 intentions are explicitly marked as the author's expansion of Tim's lens and need his steer.
The background = cloud-allowed policy is noted as permission, but the control-flow wiring for a background-cloud lane is flagged as not yet built.
run_reduce output is not automatically persisted to an addressed record, leaving a gap for a standing consolidation organ.
gpu_util-variant swaps (e.g., swarm-16k vs voice-64k) are operator-driven config+restart actions, not automatic today.
notes: A fabric-layer inventory arguing the Company already has the embedding, mining, clustering, and VRAM-governance seams the memory merge needs, with the 16 GB card as the governing constraint.
questions: What is the exact output dimension of Qwen3-Embedding-8B in this registry?
How is the background-cloud lane implemented when the local card is busy?
Where and how will cluster/consolidation outputs from run_reduce be persisted as addressed records?
What is the re-embedding scheduling policy when the fabric-grade embedder must evict the resident brain?
Can the gpu_util-variant / mode_loadout swap be automated, or does it remain operator-driven?
Which tests or measurements back the constitutions' 'VERIFIED' claims about co-residency and KV budgets?
apparent_themes: Configurable data over hardcoded model wiring
Fail-loud guard contracts for embeddings and chat
Resource governance with live VRAM measurement and governed eviction
Resident-first local cognition with optional cloud background
VRAM ceiling as the non-negotiable design bound
intention_signals: The file is trying to show that Tim's 'do a lot more with local models' intention is already structurally supported by the existing fabric layer, and that the merge should focus on scaling existing seams within the 16 GB resource envelope rather than inventing new model plumbing.
novelty: The detailed cross-mapping of merge-scale demands (re-embed 75k exchanges + 114k chunks, mine every exchange, consolidate/cluster) onto specific existing functions and files, combined with explicit VRAM arithmetic and a governed-swap framing.
contribution: A structured inventory of fabric/model-layer seams, contracts, and constraints relevant to merging episodic memory into the Company, plus a scaling-intention frame and a set of open design questions.
summary_for_embedding: A code-grounded read of the Company's local-model layer that maps existing embedding, mining, clustering, and VRAM-governance seams to the demands of merging episodic memory, emphasizing the 16 GB card as the binding constraint.
intention_for_embedding: To identify which existing fabric/cognition/resource-manager seams the merged memory build can reuse and what must scale or be scheduled around a single 16 GB GPU.
